factors.summary: Cost actions are focused on restructuring and efficiency, with some savings achieved.

evidence.text: General corporate expense increased $11.9 million and $59.6 million in the second quarter and first six months of 2015, respectively, of which $20.7 million relates to the realignment of internal resources in the first quarter of 2015, and increases in people costs, litigation and regulatory compliance expenses, as well as professional fees.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with a significant increase in cash provided by operating activities.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities in the six months ended June 30, 2015 increased by $65.0 million over the prior year, due to $36.1 million growth in Net Income, adjusted for the Brazil impairment, and improvements in working capital.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, reflecting investment in new products and technology infrastructure.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures in the first half of 2015 increased by $17.5 million from the same period in 2014 as we are continuing to invest in new products and technology infrastructure.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable, with a strong credit rating and available credit facilities.

evidence.text: At June 30, 2015, 77% of our debt was fixed-rate debt and 23% was effectively variable-rate debt. [...] Our leverage ratio was 1.64 at June 30, 2015. None of these covenants are considered restrictive to our operations, and we were in compliance with all debt covenants.

factors.summary: Unit volumes and transaction counts are growing, particularly in Online Information Solutions and Mortgage Solutions, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Core credit report transaction volume increased 49% in the second quarter of 2015 and 47% in first six months of 2015, compared to the prior year, due to increased volumes to direct to consumer resellers, complemented by revenue growth in mortgage resellers, financial services, and auto verticals, as well as a slight increase in the commercial credit and compliance businesses.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets include mortgage, auto, government, and pre-employment verticals, while international markets face challenges due to geopolitical and economic factors.

evidence.text: This broad-based growth was driven by the increase in direct to consumer reseller revenue volume, strong mortgage market activity and overall increases in financial services, auto, government and pre-employment verticals.

factors.summary: Management expects long-term organic revenue growth between 6% and 8% and earnings per share growth at a faster rate than revenue.

evidence.text: Over the long term, we expect that our ongoing investments in new product innovation, business execution, enterprise growth initiatives, technology infrastructure, and continuous process improvement will enable us to deliver long-term average organic revenue growth ranging between 6% and 8% with additional growth of 1% to 2% derived from strategic acquisitions consistent with our long term business strategy. We also expect to grow earnings per share at a somewhat faster rate than revenue over time as a result of both operating and financial leverage.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include weaker foreign exchange rates negatively impacting international revenue and profit, and geopolitical factors affecting international operations.

evidence.text: In addition, weaker foreign exchange rates, compared to the prior year, will negatively impact growth in revenue and profit when reported in U.S. dollars. The environment continues to be challenging as various countries address their particular political, fiscal and economic issues.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on new product innovation, business execution, and strategic acquisitions to drive growth.

evidence.text: Over the long term, we expect that our ongoing investments in new product innovation, business execution, enterprise growth initiatives, technology infrastructure, and continuous process improvement will enable us to deliver long-term average organic revenue growth ranging between 6% and 8% with additional growth of 1% to 2% derived from strategic acquisitions consistent with our long term business strategy.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange rates and geopolitical factors impacting international operations, as well as economic conditions affecting demand for credit services.

evidence.text: In addition, weaker foreign exchange rates, compared to the prior year, will negatively impact growth in revenue and profit when reported in U.S. dollars. The environment continues to be challenging as various countries address their particular political, fiscal and economic issues.

factors.summary: The company's margin trajectory shows mixed performance, with Personal Solutions experiencing declining operating margins due to higher technology and marketing expenses, while USIS exhibits margin expansion driven by strong revenue growth but also faces margin decline from geographic mix.

evidence.text: Operating margin decreased in the second quarter and first six months of 2015, respectively, as compared to prior year, due to higher technology and marketing expenses.

USIS operating margin increased significantly in the second quarter and first six months of 2015 as compared to prior year. Margin expansion resulted from strong revenue growth over the second quarter and first six months of 2014. Operating margin decreased in the second quarter and first six months of 2015, as compared to prior year. The decline resulted from geographic mix.

factors.summary: Management confidence is evident through continued investment in operations, strategic acquisitions, and strong financial positioning. The company's actions, such as share repurchases and dividend payouts, further demonstrate confidence in future growth and financial stability.

evidence.text: We continue to generate substantial cash from operating activities and remain in a strong financial position, with resources available for reinvestment in existing businesses, strategic acquisitions and managing our capital structure to meet short- and long-term objectives.

We repurchased 1.9 million shares of our common stock on the open market for $ 182.2 million during the first half of 2015. At June 30, 2015, $ 681.4 million was available for future purchases of common stock under our share repurchase authorization. We paid out $69.0 million or $0.58 per share in dividends to our shareholders during the first half of 2015.

factors.summary: The company reports positive organic revenue growth, driven by subscription and breach revenues in certain regions, and expects long-term average organic revenue growth between 6% and 8%. This outlook is supported by investments in new product innovation, business execution, and strategic acquisitions.

evidence.text: Revenue increased 7% and 6% for the second quarter and first six months of 2015, respectively, as compared to the prior year. Local currency revenue grew 8% and 7% in the second quarter and first six months of 2015, respectively, principally due to the growth in subscription revenue in Canada and the U.K., as well as breach revenue in the U.S.

Over the long term, we expect that our ongoing investments in new product innovation, business execution, enterprise growth initiatives, technology infrastructure, and continuous process improvement will enable us to deliver long-term average organic revenue growth ranging between 6% and 8% with additional growth of 1% to 2% derived from strategic acquisitions consistent with our long term business strategy.

factors.summary: Demand for Equifax's services is showing mixed trends. While there is an increase in demand supported by growth in subscription and breach revenues, particularly in Canada and the U.K., the international demand remains challenging due to geopolitical and economic factors. The U.S. market is expected to experience modest growth driven by economic activity and consumer credit, though there are concerns about weaker foreign exchange rates impacting revenue growth when reported in U.S. dollars.

evidence.text: Revenue increased 7% and 6% for the second quarter and first six months of 2015, respectively, as compared to the prior year. Local currency revenue grew 8% and 7% in the second quarter and first six months of 2015, respectively, principally due to the growth in subscription revenue in Canada and the U.K., as well as breach revenue in the U.S.

In 2015, in the United States, we continue to expect modest growth in overall economic activity and consumer credit, including in the second half of the year. We also expect mortgage market origination activity to be up slightly over 10% for all of 2015. Consistent with our expectations, mortgage market growth was strong in the first half of 2015, but is expected to be up only slightly in the second half of the year. Internationally, the environment continues to be challenging as various countries address their particular political, fiscal and economic issues. In addition, weaker foreign exchange rates, compared to the prior year, will negatively impact growth in revenue and profit when reported in U.S. dollars.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being enhanced through a combination of increased dividends and substantial treasury stock repurchases. The company has demonstrated a strong commitment to returning value to shareholders by consistently repurchasing shares and increasing dividend payouts.

evidence.text: We increased our quarterly dividend from $0.25 per share to $0.29 per share [...] We repurchased approximately 1.9 million of our common shares [...] at an average price of $94.94 per share.

We repurchased 1.9 million shares of our common stock on the open market for $ 182.2 million during the first half of 2015. At June 30, 2015, $ 681.4 million was available for future purchases of common stock under our share repurchase authorization. We paid out $69.0 million or $0.58 per share in dividends to our shareholders during the first half of 2015.
